evals.expected_output: Reads the journal, compares plugin version to the journaled version, then reconciles per file using the recorded sha256 as the third merge point: managed files whose current hash equals the recorded hash are overwritten with the new render and re-journaled; every re-render uses the journaled answers without re-interviewing; finishes with a doctor re-run and a journal stamp bump.

assertions.name: three-way
assertions.description: Reconciliation compares RECORDED (journal sha256), CURRENT (disk), and NEWRENDER (new template with journaled answers).

assertions.name: unmodified-overwritten
assertions.description: A managed file with CURRENT == RECORDED is overwritten without prompting.

assertions.name: no-reinterview
assertions.description: The upgrade renders from journal.answers and never asks the interview screens again.

evals.prompt: Upgrade agentic-os. I have local edits in one of the managed hook files.
evals.expected_output: Detects CURRENT != RECORDED for that hook, shows a diff (template-old to template-new when the old render is recoverable from a release tag, otherwise a clearly labeled CURRENT to new diff), and asks before touching the file — keeping it marks it owner: user.

assertions.name: never-silent
assertions.description: The user-modified file is never overwritten without an explicit user choice.

assertions.name: labeled-diff
assertions.description: The diff shown is labeled as template-old→new or CURRENT→new so the user knows whether their edits are mixed into it.

evals.prompt: Upgrade the scaffold. CLAUDE.md has a managed agentic-os block plus my own content above and below it.
evals.expected_output: Replaces only the content between the managed block markers wholesale with the new render, leaves everything outside the markers byte-identical, and updates the journal entry for the block.

assertions.name: block-scoped
assertions.description: Only the text between the managed markers changes; user content outside them is untouched.

evals.prompt: /agentic-upgrade — the installed plugin version is older than the journaled version.
evals.expected_output: Stops and escalates: a downgrade is a human decision, not something the upgrade reconciles automatically.

assertions.name: downgrade-escalates
assertions.description: The response stops on NEW < OLD and asks the human instead of reconciling.
